heading: Preemption; State flexibility; construction
label: 1191 Preemption; State flexibility; construction
notes: Amendments1996—Subsec. (c). , substituted “Except as provided in , nothing” for “Nothing”.Pub. L. 104–204, § 603(b)(1)section 1185 of this title

children.heading: Continued applicability of State law with respect to health insurance issuers
children.label: a Continued applicability of State law with respect to health insurance issuers

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2) and except as provided in subsection (b), this part shall not be construed to supersede any provision of State law which establishes, implements, or continues in effect any standard or requirement solely relating to health insurance issuers in connection with group health insurance coverage except to the extent that such standard or requirement prevents the application of a requirement of this part.

children.heading: Continued preemption with respect to group health plans
children.label: 2 Continued preemption with respect to group health plans
children.text: Nothing in this part shall be construed to affect or modify the provisions of with respect to group health plans.section 1144 of this title

children.heading: Special rules in case of portability requirements
children.label: b Special rules in case of portability requirements

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), the provisions of this part relating to health insurance coverage offered by a health insurance issuer supersede any provision of State law which establishes, implements, or continues in effect a standard or requirement applicable to imposition of a preexisting condition exclusion specifically governed by which differs from the standards or requirements specified in such section.section 1181 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: substitutes for the reference to “6-month period” in a reference to any shorter period of time;section 1181(a)(1) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: substitutes for the reference to “12 months” and “18 months” in a reference to any shorter period of time;section 1181(a)(2) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: substitutes for the references to “63 days” in sections 1181(c)(2)(A) and (d)(4)(A) of this title a reference to any greater number of days;1So in original. does not contain subpars.1Section 1181(d)(4) of this title

children.label: D
children.text: substitutes for the reference to “30-day period” in sections 1181(b)(2) and (d)(1) of this title a reference to any greater period;2So in original. does not refer to a 30-day period.2Section 1181(b)(2) of this title

children.label: E
children.text: prohibits the imposition of any preexisting condition exclusion in cases not described in or expands the exceptions described in such section;section 1181(d) of this title

children.label: F
children.text: requires special enrollment periods in addition to those required under ; orsection 1181(f) of this title

children.label: G
children.text: reduces the maximum period permitted in an affiliation period under section 1181(g)(1)(B) of this title.3So in original. Probably should be “1181(g)(1)(C)”.3

children.heading: Rules of construction
children.label: c Rules of construction
children.text: Except as provided in , nothing in this part shall be construed as requiring a group health plan or health insurance coverage to provide specific benefits under the terms of such plan or coverage.section 1185 of this title

children.heading: State law
children.label: 1 State law
children.text: The term “State law” includes all laws, decisions, rules, regulations, or other State action having the effect of law, of any State. A law of the United States applicable only to the District of Columbia shall be treated as a State law rather than a law of the United States.

children.heading: State
children.label: 2 State
children.text: The term “State” includes a State, the Northern Mariana Islands, any political subdivisions of a State or such Islands, or any agency or instrumentality of either.
